title: U Mass - Glucose
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8ddjg","text":"Summary: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e4mou","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bl219","text":"This experiment measures blood or plasma glucose concentration using Analox GM9 Glucose Analyzer or GM7 Micro-Stat Rapid Multi-assay Analyzer.  The measurement is based on the rate of oxygen uptake in the reaction between sample glucose and glucose oxidase.  Plasma glucose levels are elevated (i.e., hyperglycemia) in obesity, insulin resistance, and type 2 diabetes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

If storing samples, keep blood and plasma samples at –20º C or at -70ºC for long-term storage. Avoid freeze/thaw cycles. steps: Check waste and fill glucose reagent reservoir.
Press “Enter”, and then “No” to cycle the Analox Analyzer.
When cycling is finished, wait for reading to return to ~0 mg/dl (±1mg/dl).
Using precision pipet, inject 10 µl of standard solution.
When value is reached, accept calibration.
When reading has returned to ~0 mg/dl, inject 10 µl of standard solution, and ensure that the value is ~144 mg/dl.
Repeat steps #5~#9 until calibration value is stable.
Check calibration by injecting 5 µl of standard and multiplying reported value by 2.
The final calculated value should be ~144 mg/dl.
Instrument is now ready for sample measurement. Use 5 or 10 µl of plasma sample for glucose concentration measurement.
creator: Lili Liang
